_note: Working world dataset for map research. Coordinates are hypotheses until their placement basis, evidence, and uncertainty have been reviewed under docs/MAP-ARCHITECTURE.md; the application must not treat this file as display-ready yet.

terrain_types.name: Salt Flats
terrain_types.description: Extremely flat, reflective salt crust with minimal cover and long sight lines.

terrain_types.name: Urban Ruins
terrain_types.description: Collapsed buildings, alleys and debris fields providing dense cover and complex sight lines.

terrain_types.name: Mountain Ridge
terrain_types.description: Steep, rocky high ground with excellent observation but difficult movement.

terrain_types.name: Desert Scrub
terrain_types.description: Sparse bushes, rocks and dry soil offering limited cover and moderate sight lines.

terrain_types.name: Forest Hills
terrain_types.description: Wooded hills and valleys with heavy vegetation and broken sight lines.

regions.name: New California Republic
regions.real_world_extent_hint: Southern California, parts of Nevada, Baja California, portions of Oregon.

terminal_entry.title: NCR STATE ARCHIVE – NEW CALIFORNIA REPUBLIC
body: Representative republic established from the settlement of Shady Sands, expanding across much of pre-war southern California and adjoining territories.
At peak it incorporates multiple contiguous states reaching north toward Oregon and south toward Baja, overlaying pre-war borders rather than preserving them.
The dominant organized power in the western wasteland, projecting law, trade and military presence into former US states without restoring their exact pre-war lines.

regions.name: Mojave Wasteland
regions.real_world_extent_hint: Southern Nevada and northwest Arizona around Las Vegas and Hoover Dam.

terminal_entry.title: MOJAVE SURVEY RECORD – MOJAVE WASTELAND
body: Arid desert and canyon lands centred on the pre-war Mojave Desert and Colorado River corridor, including the greater Las Vegas area and Hoover Dam.
Territorial divisions reflect NCR, Legion and independent holdings.
Control of Mojave transport routes and Hoover Dam output is crucial for NCR expansion and rival power projection into the interior.

regions.name: Capital Wasteland
regions.real_world_extent_hint: District of Columbia, southern Maryland, northern Virginia, adjacent Pennsylvania.

terminal_entry.title: FEDERAL EMERGENCY RECORD – CAPITAL WASTELAND
body: Devastated urban and suburban zone centred on the ruins of Washington, D.C., extending into surrounding portions of Virginia, Maryland and Pennsylvania.
Post-war inhabitants reference the area as a single Capital Wasteland rather than by pre-war state names.
Contains critical pre-war infrastructure — monuments, water projects and military sites — a focal point for Enclave and Brotherhood operations.

regions.name: Commonwealth
regions.real_world_extent_hint: Boston metropolitan area and central Massachusetts.

terminal_entry.title: COMMONWEALTH GOVERNANCE FILE – MASSACHUSETTS COMMONWEALTH
body: Post-war designation for the Boston and central Massachusetts area, where city and town identities persist but formal state structures have collapsed.
The term Commonwealth is widely used in place of the pre-war state name.
Contains Diamond City, the Institute and major ruins — central to human habitation and advanced technology in the northeast.

regions.name: Appalachia
regions.real_world_extent_hint: State of West Virginia and surrounding Appalachian valleys.

terminal_entry.title: US RECLAMATION BRIEF – APPALACHIA
body: Mountainous and forested territory corresponding to pre-war West Virginia, with scattered towns, mines and industrial sites.
Post-war references use Appalachia as a regional name rather than the former state boundary.
An early test case for post-war reclamation initiatives and automated aid systems.

regions.name: Midwestern Wasteland
regions.real_world_extent_hint: Kansas, Illinois and adjacent Midwestern states.

terminal_entry.title: BROTHERHOOD STRATEGIC BRIEF – MIDWESTERN WASTELAND
body: Central U.S. plains and urban centres across Kansas, Illinois and neighbouring states, marked by collapsed agriculture and dispersed communities.
Lacks cohesive post-war state structures — scattered towns, raider territories and emergent Brotherhood enclaves.
Offers access to surviving industrial corridors and transport routes; a priority for Brotherhood expansion.

regions.name: Wendover – Misfits Nuclear Wasteland
regions.real_world_extent_hint: Wendover, Utah and West Wendover, Nevada, on the Great Salt Flats.

terminal_entry.title: LOCAL SURVEY RECORD – WENDOVER SALT FLATS REGION
body: Post-war wasteland centred on the twin communities of Wendover, Utah and West Wendover, Nevada, on the ancient shores of Lake Bonneville beside the Toana Range.
Bleached salt flats offer minimal cover and harsh exposure; nearby ranges rise sharply from the desert floor.
A frontier theatre for NCR, Brotherhood and independent actors in the Misfits: Nuclear Wasteland scenario, with vault installations and outposts embedded in the local geography.

sources.title: Fallout locations
sources.note: Marker identity cross-check; individual claims still require the cited game or guide material.

sources.title: Boneyard
sources.note: Secondary index to game and official-guide references identifying the Boneyard with Los Angeles.

sources.title: Necropolis
sources.note: Secondary index to game and official-guide references identifying Necropolis with Bakersfield.

sources.title: The Hub
sources.note: Identity and setting reference; it does not establish a real-world city.

sources.title: Lost Hills
sources.note: Records the same-name real place without proving that the fictional site is identical to it.

sources.title: Mariposa Military Base
sources.note: Collects conflicting game-map, later reference-work, and developer-inspiration evidence.

sources.title: The Glow
sources.note: Establishes the West Tek identity and relative game-map position, not a real-world point.

sources.title: The Cathedral
sources.note: Links the Cathedral to the Los Angeles and Boneyard location cluster.

sources.title: Fallout 2 CITY.TXT transcript
sources.note: Transcript of the shipped world-map city data, used for relative pixel positions rather than real-world identity.

sources.title: Fallout Bible complete
sources.note: Post-release developer reference; useful for design intent but kept distinct from in-game evidence.

sources.title: New Reno
sources.note: Secondary index to game dialogue and reference material identifying New Reno with Reno while noting game-map displacement.

sources.title: Vault City
sources.note: Includes the in-game travel-log wording and the conflicting northern Nevada map interpretation.

sources.title: New Vegas Strip
sources.note: Secondary index to game material identifying the fortified district with the surviving Las Vegas Strip.

sources.title: Goodsprings
sources.note: Secondary index to game and design references identifying the settlement with real Goodsprings.

sources.title: Nevada census-designated places
sources.note: Government geographic reference used for the Goodsprings centre point.

sources.title: Primm
sources.note: Secondary index to dialogue and game references identifying the settlement with real Primm beside Interstate 15.

sources.title: Novac
sources.note: Establishes Novac as a fictional Highway 95 settlement and keeps its roadside-sign inspirations distinct from town identity.

sources.title: Boulder City
sources.note: Secondary index to game references identifying the ruins with real Boulder City.

sources.title: Hoover Dam
sources.note: Secondary index to game material identifying the location with the real Colorado River dam.

sources.title: The Fort
sources.note: Places the fictional camp in the same geographical area as Fortification Hill east of Hoover Dam.

sources.title: Fortification Hill
sources.note: National Park Service geographic reference for the real hill and its relationship to Hoover Dam.

sources.title: Camp McCarran
sources.note: Secondary index to game dialogue identifying the base with the former Las Vegas airport.

sources.title: Harry Reid International Airport
sources.note: Aeronautical geographic reference used for the airport centre point.

sources.title: Cottonwood Cove
sources.note: Secondary index identifying the Legion landing with the real Cottonwood Cove resort area.

sources.title: Cottonwood Cove
sources.note: Bureau of Reclamation geographic reference used for the cove point.

sources.title: Nelson
sources.note: Secondary index to game material identifying the occupied settlement with real Nelson, Nevada.

sources.title: Jacobstown
sources.note: Places the fictional refuge in the Mount Charleston area and associates its lodge with the same-area mountain retreat.

sources.title: Black Mountain
sources.note: Places the fictional broadcast site in the same area as the real Black Mountain in Sloan Canyon.

locations.name: Vault 13
locations.real_world_anchor: Undetermined mountain location in southern California

placement.method_note: Legacy candidate retained for research continuity only; its direction from Shady Sands conflicts with the submitted description and requires a same-map anchor fit.

evidence.note: Confirms Vault 13 as a Fallout world-map location but does not establish a real-world site.

terminal_entry.title: Vault-Tec Secure Archive – VAULT 13
body: A long-term Vault-Tec habitation facility embedded in mountain rock somewhere in southern California.
Sealed at the Great War in 2077; population maintained under closed ecosystem protocols dependent on a single integrated water purification chip.
c.2161 — systemic water processing failure triggers dispatch of a surface surveyor, leading to extensive contact with external settlements.

locations.name: Shady Sands
locations.real_world_anchor: Undetermined desert location in southern California

placement.method_note: Legacy candidate retained for research continuity only; the submitted point conflicts with stated relative directions and is not ready for display.

evidence.note: Confirms Shady Sands as a Fallout world-map location but not a real-world point.

terminal_entry.title: NCR CIVIC DATABASE – SHADY SANDS (FOUNDING NODE)
body: Agricultural and civic hub established by former Vault 15 residents at an unresolved location in the southern California interior.
c.2161 — a small but politically ambitious township experimenting with representative governance and codified law.
Later formalized as the capital of the New California Republic; origin point for NCR legal codes and expansion policy.

placement.method_note: The city identity is explicit; the marker represents the Los Angeles metropolitan centre rather than an exact site.

evidence.note: Identifies the Boneyard as the remains of Los Angeles.

terminal_entry.title: COASTAL RUIN SURVEY – THE BONEYARD (LOS ANGELES)
body: A vast cemetery of collapsed skyscrapers and suburban sprawl corresponding to the former Los Angeles metropolitan area.
Fragmented communities and militias occupy districts such as Adytum.
A key source of salvage technology and printed knowledge, making control of habitable zones a recurring flashpoint.

placement.method_note: The settlement is well established, but the proposed point contradicts its stated direction from Shady Sands and no real-world city identity was verified.

evidence.note: Establishes the Hub as a major New California settlement without identifying a real-world city.

terminal_entry.title: CARAVAN EXCHANGE RECORD – THE HUB
body: A major trading settlement linking the communities of New California.
Its precise real-world placement remains unresolved pending a consistent fit to reviewed world-map anchors.

placement.method_note: The city identity is explicit; the marker represents central Bakersfield rather than a surviving structure.

evidence.note: Identifies Necropolis as the post-war remains of Bakersfield.

terminal_entry.title: CENTRAL VALLEY RUIN SURVEY – NECROPOLIS
body: A post-war settlement occupying the remains of Bakersfield.
Its urban identity is firm even though an exact district-level marker is not justified.

locations.name: Lost Hills
locations.real_world_anchor: Candidate area around Lost Hills, California

placement.method_note: A same-name real place is a useful locus, but identity is not explicit enough for a reviewed point; the broad radius preserves that distinction.

evidence.note: Notes a same-name California place but does not prove exact fictional identity.

terminal_entry.title: BROTHERHOOD ARCHIVE – LOST HILLS
body: The Brotherhood of Steel headquarters in New California.
Its relationship to the same-name real-world place remains a provisional geographic hypothesis.

placement.method_note: The game-map position, Fort Ord design inspiration, and later Clouds Rest or Yosemite-area reference cannot be collapsed into one defensible coordinate.

evidence.note: Establishes the military base as a Fallout location in central California.
Records distinct game-map, later reference-work, and developer-inspiration loci.

terminal_entry.title: MILITARY SITE RECORD – MARIPOSA
body: A pre-war military installation associated with forced evolutionary virus research.
Its identity is established, but competing geographic evidence prevents a responsible public marker.

locations.name: The Glow
locations.real_world_anchor: Undetermined West Tek site in southern California

placement.method_note: Relative distances on the game map are useful only after Vault 13 and other same-map anchors are reconciled.

evidence.note: Identifies the Glow as the ruined West Tek research facility.
Provides game-map relationships to Vault 13 and the Boneyard without fixing a real-world point.

terminal_entry.title: WEST TEK EXCLUSION RECORD – THE GLOW
body: The irradiated remains of a West Tek research facility.
Its real-world point remains withheld until the Fallout world-map anchor fit is internally consistent.

placement.method_note: Sources place it in the Los Angeles and Boneyard cluster, but do not justify an exact surviving building.

evidence.note: Associates the Cathedral and its vault with the Los Angeles ruins.

terminal_entry.title: LOS ANGELES RUIN RECORD – THE CATHEDRAL
body: A prominent structure within the Los Angeles ruins and the wider Boneyard cluster.
The metropolitan placement is useful, while the exact building remains unresolved.

locations.name: Arroyo
locations.real_world_anchor: Fictional locale west of the Klamath Falls locus

placement.method_note: The developer reference says the founders headed north, groups Arroyo with a Northern California drought, and calls the locale fictional. The shipped map places it nearly level with and west of Klamath Falls; the point represents the broad California–Oregon frontier overlap, not a named town.

evidence.note: Records a northward founding journey, associates Arroyo with a Northern California drought, and states that Tim Cain regarded Arroyo as fictional.
Places Arroyo at 184,133 and Klamath at 373,122 on the shipped world map.

terminal_entry.title: TRIBAL ARCHIVE – ARROYO
body: A secluded fictional settlement founded by the exiled Vault Dweller and companions somewhere along the northern California–southern Oregon frontier.
The regional hypothesis is useful, but no surviving real-world town is established and the marker must retain broad uncertainty.

placement.method_note: The Reno identity is explicit; the point represents the real metropolitan centre because the Fallout 2 world-map marker is displaced into the Sierra Nevada.

evidence.note: Identifies New Reno with the surviving pre-war city of Reno and records the game-map displacement.
Places New Reno at 923,922 on the shipped world map for same-map comparisons.

terminal_entry.title: ORGANIZED CRIME DOSSIER – NEW RENO
body: Post-war entertainment and vice hub corresponding to pre-war Reno, dominated by competing crime families and casinos.
A major producer and distributor of chems, arms and illicit services for the western wasteland.
High opportunity for covert negotiation offset by extreme volatility and violent conflict.

locations.name: Vault City
locations.real_world_anchor: Broad border region northeast of Reno

placement.method_note: The shipped map places Vault City north and east of New Reno. A four-anchor affine check points into northwest Nevada but has longitude residuals exceeding one degree; the border-centred candidate and wide radius preserve the in-game travel log's Northern California wording.

evidence.locator: Area 04 Vault City and Areas 02, 07, 13, and 14
evidence.note: Places Vault City at 1223,322 relative to Klamath, New Reno, Redding, and San Francisco.

evidence.locator: Notes and Vault City travel log
evidence.note: The world-map interpretation indicates northern Nevada, while the in-game travel log describes the city within Northern California.

terminal_entry.title: VAULT 8 CIVIC RECORD – VAULT CITY
body: An advanced, insular settlement founded by the inhabitants of Vault 8.
Its broad borderland placement follows the game map while preserving the conflicting Northern California travel-log description.

terminal_entry.title: BROTHERHOOD FIELD LOG – CANYON
body: A small agricultural town on the former central-U.S. plains, used as a local recruitment and supply point.
Early Brotherhood Midwest engagements here establish a foothold against raider and mutant threats.
A staging ground for deeper incursions into the Midwestern Wasteland.

terminal_entry.title: CAPITAL WASTELAND TRAVEL NODE – MEGATON
body: A fortified shanty-town built around an undetonated atomic bomb in a former highway crater on the outskirts of ruined Washington.
Authority is exercised by a sheriff and council balancing a bomb-centric cult with merchant interests.
An early resupply and social hub for travellers entering the Capital Wasteland.

placement.method_note: The district identity is explicit; the marker represents the central Las Vegas Strip rather than one gate or casino.

evidence.note: Identifies the fortified New Vegas district with the surviving Las Vegas Strip.

terminal_entry.title: MOJAVE SECURITY DOSSIER – NEW VEGAS STRIP
body: A fortified entertainment corridor corresponding to the pre-war Las Vegas Strip, restored under Mr. House and securitron police forces.
Neutral ground where NCR, Legion and independents compete via diplomacy, gambling and covert action.
Control of the Strip confers leverage over Hoover Dam negotiations and regional resource flows.

placement.method_note: The town identity is explicit; the marker uses the government geographic centre rather than an individual building.

evidence.note: Identifies the settlement with real Goodsprings and notes the similar street layout.
Provides the geographic centre used for the candidate point.

terminal_entry.title: MOJAVE SETTLEMENT RECORD – GOODSPRINGS
body: A small community occupying the real Goodsprings townsite southwest of Las Vegas.
Its town identity is firm; the marker does not claim an exact surviving structure.

placement.method_note: The settlement identity and Interstate 15 setting are explicit; the marker represents the real town centre.

evidence.note: Identifies the settlement with real Primm in Clark County beside Interstate 15.

terminal_entry.title: MOJAVE SETTLEMENT RECORD – PRIMM
body: A transport settlement corresponding to real Primm on Interstate 15.
Its town-level identity is explicit, while individual surviving structures are not georeferenced here.

locations.name: Novac
locations.real_world_anchor: Fictional Highway 95 settlement near the Nevada Route 165 corridor

placement.method_note: Novac is fictional. The point marks a broad Highway 95 corridor near the Nelson junction, not the separate real-world sites that inspired its dinosaur sign.

evidence.note: Establishes Novac as a fictional motel settlement and distinguishes multiple sign inspirations from town identity.
Places the settlement on Highway 95 south of the Gibson scrapyard and north of the southern river corridor.

terminal_entry.title: MOJAVE ROADSIDE RECORD – NOVAC
body: A fictional motel settlement on the Highway 95 corridor, recognised by a surviving dinosaur sign.
The corridor placement is deliberately broad because no real town identity is established.

placement.method_note: The city identity is explicit; the marker represents central Boulder City rather than the game's compressed ruin layout.

evidence.note: Identifies the ruins with real Boulder City southeast of Las Vegas.

terminal_entry.title: MOJAVE RUIN RECORD – BOULDER CITY
body: Ruins corresponding to real Boulder City on the western approach to Hoover Dam.
The marker represents the city rather than a specific battlefield position.

placement.method_note: The structure identity is explicit; the point represents the dam complex at the Colorado River.

evidence.note: Identifies the game location with the real dam on the Nevada–Arizona border.

terminal_entry.title: COLORADO INFRASTRUCTURE RECORD – HOOVER DAM
body: The surviving Colorado River dam and the principal power and water objective of the Mojave campaign.
Its real-world structure provides the strongest exact anchor in this batch.

placement.method_note: The camp is placed in the Fortification Hill area overlooking Hoover Dam; the summit is a regional locus, not an exact camp coordinate.

evidence.note: Places the camp in the same geographical area as Fortification Hill east of Hoover Dam.
Confirms the real hill's position and overlook relationship to the dam area.

terminal_entry.title: LEGION FIELD RECORD – THE FORT
body: A fortified camp on the Arizona side of the Colorado River in the Fortification Hill area.
The hill is a strong regional counterpart, but the camp itself remains a fictional placement.

locations.name: Camp McCarran
locations.real_world_anchor: Harry Reid International Airport, Las Vegas

placement.method_note: The base explicitly occupies the former Las Vegas airport; the point represents the airfield centre rather than a terminal entrance.

evidence.note: Identifies the base as the former airport beside New Vegas.
Provides the published airfield reference point used for the marker.

terminal_entry.title: NCR BASE RECORD – CAMP MCCARRAN
body: An NCR base occupying the former Las Vegas airport immediately south of the Strip.
The airfield identity is explicit; the marker uses its published centre point.

placement.method_note: The landing is identified with the real cove and resort area; the point uses the published geographic location.

evidence.note: Identifies the Legion landing with the real Cottonwood Cove resort area.
Provides the published cove coordinate used for the marker.

terminal_entry.title: COLORADO LANDING RECORD – COTTONWOOD COVE
body: A river landing corresponding to the real Cottonwood Cove area on Lake Mohave.
The geographic identity is direct even though the fictional fortifications are not individually mapped.

placement.method_note: The occupied settlement is identified with real Nelson; the marker represents the small town area rather than a specific structure.

evidence.note: Identifies the game settlement with real Nelson southeast of Las Vegas.

terminal_entry.title: MOJAVE SETTLEMENT RECORD – NELSON
body: An occupied settlement corresponding to real Nelson in southern Nevada.
The town identity is explicit; the marker retains town-level precision.

placement.method_note: The refuge occupies the Mount Charleston area and uses a same-area lodge counterpart; the point is a mountain-area locus rather than a literal building match.

evidence.note: Places Jacobstown in the Mount Charleston area and associates its lodge with a real mountain retreat.

terminal_entry.title: MOUNTAIN REFUGE RECORD – JACOBSTOWN
body: A fictional refuge in the wooded Mount Charleston highlands west of Las Vegas.
The mountain area is well constrained, while the exact lodge identity remains inferential.

placement.method_note: The broadcast site occupies the same area as the real Black Mountain; the summit point is a regional counterpart rather than a proven tower coordinate.

evidence.note: Places the fictional site in the same area as the real Black Mountain in Sloan Canyon.

terminal_entry.title: MOJAVE BROADCAST RECORD – BLACK MOUNTAIN
body: A fictional broadcast position associated with the real Black Mountain southeast of Las Vegas.
The summit anchors the broad area without asserting an exact surviving tower site.

terminal_entry.title: COMMONWEALTH CIVIC RECORD – DIAMOND CITY
body: A major walled settlement built into and around the preserved structure of a pre-war baseball stadium.
Administered by a mayor and civic institutions balancing security with trade.
The symbolic and practical centre of human habitation in the Commonwealth.

terminal_entry.title: VAULT-TEC OPERATIONAL BRIEF – VAULT 76
body: A Control Vault with baseline social conditions, designated to open shortly after the Great War to spearhead reclamation of Appalachia.
Overseer directives focus on survey, resettlement and reactivation of surviving infrastructure.
The origin point for multiple reclamation initiatives and early regional governance experiments.

locations.name: Wendover Town Ruins
locations.real_world_anchor: Wendover, Utah, and West Wendover, Nevada

terminal_entry.title: LOCAL SETTLEMENT LOG – WENDOVER TOWN RUINS
body: Twin border towns straddling the line between former Utah and Nevada, reduced to scattered structures and half-functioning neon remnants along the salt flat edge.
Built on an ancient beach of Lake Bonneville and pressed against the rising Toana Range, the town offers limited vertical cover and long lines of sight across the flats.
A staging and trading nexus for wasteland travellers, linking vault installations, NCR patrols and independent scavenger camps.

locations.name: Great Salt Flats Expanse
locations.real_world_anchor: Bonneville Salt Flats east of Wendover, Utah

terminal_entry.title: ENVIRONMENTAL SURVEY – GREAT SALT FLATS
body: A bleached, featureless expanse of salt crust stretching east of Wendover, offering little cover and extreme visual exposure to any moving parties.
Sudden dust storms, mirage effects and reflective glare complicate navigation; vehicle travel leaves easy-to-track trails.
Frequently used as a kill-zone and ambush field; vault dwellers and NCR patrols cross it at considerable risk.

locations.name: Toana Range Overlook
locations.real_world_anchor: Foothills of the Toana Range south of Wendover, Utah

terminal_entry.title: TACTICAL SITE LOG – TOANA RANGE OVERLOOK
body: An elevated outpost and observation point carved into the lower slopes of the Toana Range, overlooking the town and salt flats below.
Provides long-range fields of fire and early warning against movements across the flats; bunkers and makeshift antennae dot the ridgeline.
Favoured by organized factions such as NCR units and Brotherhood detachments for coordinating operations in the Wendover theatre.

locations.name: Local Vault Installation
locations.real_world_anchor: Subsurface facility inferred beneath salt flats near Wendover, Utah

terminal_entry.title: VAULT-ADJACENT INCIDENT RECORD – WENDOVER INSTALLATION
body: An unofficial Vault-class installation embedded beneath the salt crust east of Wendover, a narrative anchor for vault dwellers in the scenario.
Access routes are concealed beneath maintenance shafts and collapsed pre-war test facilities; multiple factions contest interior control.
The primary origin point for vault dwellers entering the Wendover theatre.

connections.description: Cracked highway leading east out of Wendover into the open salt flats.
Steep path from town edge up into the Toana Ridge outpost.
Concealed subsurface access from a maintenance block to the vault installation beneath the flats.

event_hooks.summary: Recurring ambushes of NCR caravans crossing the salt flats east of Wendover.
Attempted breach of the Wendover vault installation by raider forces, with NCR response.
Large-scale salvage operation into the Boneyard ruins for pre-war data and equipment.
